article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

reasoning: Returned an empty array despite multiple clear instances of biased author prose and even wrapped it in markdown fences. No attribution violations (nothing flagged), but the model missed significant bias in the author's own voice.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: No quoted or sourced language was flagged because nothing was flagged at all; thus no attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple clear author-prose biases: e.g., 'ghoulish... dark cloud' (sensational/subjective), 'knocked the lazy certainty out of almost everybody' (subjective/mudslinging), 'It was a welcome change' (opinion as fact), '“National conversations” are usually efforts to bully everyone...' (word choice/opinion as fact), jab at Giuliani 'who did not lose his lazy certainty' (ad hominem), and 'Liberals ... are often rather obtuse' (mudslinging).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias items identified, so label accuracy cannot be meaningfully assessed; scored neutral.
